def: s. m. Recoil.

ety: S. पर्शु+dim. aff. lī = fem. fr. S. aff. रः
def: s.f. A rib; the præcordia
more: n. f. S. पार्श्व, Pālī. phāsulikā . A rib; the prœcordia. Ek jānwar aslī, jiske haḍḍī na paslī . Riddle. Ans. Joṅkh a leech. Nor bones nor ribs and yet a live animal. paslī phaṛaknā , v. n. To thrill (the ribs). A sign of an impending visit from an absent friend.
s. f. A rib, the præcordia. پسلي پهڙکني paslī phaṛaknī, A thrilling over the ribs, is applied to a perception of the condition of an absent person.
subs: v.n. To feel a quivering or thrilling sensation over the ribs (regarded as a sign of an impending visit from an absent friend, or as a perception of his or her condition)
s.m. Side; a tract of country.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀁𑀲𑀼𑀮𑀺𑀆 (paṃsuliā), from Sanskrit पर्शु (parśu), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *párćuš, from Proto-Indo-European *pérḱus.

def: direct plural of پَسْلی (paslī)
oblique plural of پَسْلی (paslī)
vocative plural of پَسْلی (paslī)
part. Remaining behind; a remaining person, a surviver.
v.n. To have a flow of milk (an animal); the milk to descend (to the udder).
more: v. n. S. पयस milk. To come down (the milk from the udders). Bhaiṅs der meṅ pāstī hai .

def: Pasni (a city in Gwadar district, Balochistan, Pakistan)
wety: From Baluchi پسنی.

def: s. m. Evasion, prevarication, hesitation. پس وپيش کرنا pas-o-pesh karnā, v. a. To prevaricate, boggle, demur, doubt.
adj. See s.v. pas for compounds of which that word is the first member.
v. n. 1. To decline. 2. To fall in the rear, to slink behind, to retreat, to flee, to desist from an attempt.
v. a. To repulse.
passport
wety: Borrowed from English passport.

def: n. m. S. पोष्यपुत्र An adopted son ( le-pālak ). Apnā dhiyā, pūtā nahī chhaiṅh, posh-put lelanhī achhī . Tir. It is best to adopt a child when you have none of your own.
more: s. m. Adopted son. پسپوت کرنا pus-pūt karnā, To adopt.

def: s. m. 1. A follower. 2. Dependants. 3. Train, equipage.
s. f. Following.
adj. Low, humble; abject, mean, base, vile; lower; below
more: adj. Low; mean; base. past khayālāt' , n. m. ( Rare ) Low ideas. past-himmat , adj. Low-minded; mean-spirited; without courage. past karnā , v. a. To put down; defeat; baffle; vanquish. past honā , v. n. To be vanquished, overcome, done up.

adj. Low, abject, lower, below. پست فطرت past-fit̤rat, adj. Of inferiour understanding, low, base. پست همت past himmat, adj. Low-minded, mean-spirited, unambitious. پست همتي past-himmat-ī, s. f. Low-mindedness, mean-spiritedness. پست وبلندديکهنا past o buland dekhnā, To consider and take precautions against the vicissitudes of fortune.
subs: adj. Of inferior understanding; of a mean disposition; low-natured; base, vile
adj. & s.m. Low in stature; a dwarf
v.t. To lower, put down; to render abject, to humiliate; to defeat, vanquish
v.n. To look above and below; to look about (one); to consider and take precautions against the vicissitudes of fortune
adj. Low-minded; mean-spirited; without courage; unambitious
v.n. To be humbled, be put down; to be humiliated; to be overcome, be vanquished.

def: pasta
wety: Borrowed from Persian پاستا (pâstâ) or English pasta, both from Italian pasta, ultimately from Ancient Greek παστά (pastá).

def: adj. Past, former, old, ancient, of yore.
v.n. To regret, &c. = paćhtānā, q.v. v.n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.
more: v. n. To regret. See پچهتانا pachhtānā.

def: corr. of paćhtānā, q.v. v.n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.
more: v. n. To repent: see پچهتانا pachhtānā.

def: s.f. Lowness, inferiority; humility; baseness.
s.m. = پشو paśu, q.v.
more: s. m. A beast, an animal. پسوپالک pasu-pālak, s. m. A cowherd.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पिस्सू (pissū).

def: — see پشو paśu.

s.m. Cattle, brute, beast, animal (neat-cattle, horses, goats, sheep, asses, and dogs); a young buffalo (local); an animal to be immolated, a victim; a mere animal in sacred things, an uninitiated person; a simpleton, blockhead; — a subordinate deity and one of Śiva's followers; — the soul (according to the tenets of Pāśupatas)
more: n. m. 1. Cattle ( chaupāyā ) 2. (Tir). A young buffalo. Hamar pashū āv biāet . Tir. My buffalo is about to calve. 3. A simpleton; fool; blockhead.
subs: paśu-pālak, s.m. Keeper of herds, herdsman, cattle-rearer
s.m. 'Lord of the animals,' or 'lord of a servant named Paśu', an epithet of Śiva; — paśu-pati-nāth, s.m. An epithet of Śivā
adj. Like an animal; brutish; uncivilized
s.m. A fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus (which grows in pools frequented by cattle)
s.m. The characteristics of cattle, action or function of animals, treatment of cattle or animals; promiscuous cohabitation; the marrying of widows
s.m. 'Monarch of the beasts,' a lion
s.m. Cord for tethering cattle, tether for animals
s.m. The hair of an animal
s.f. The act of animal sacrifice; acting like cattle, copulation
s.f. A parody of the holy verse of the Vedas whispered in the ear of an animal about to be sacrificed
adj. & adv. Animal-like, brutish, irrational; — like an animal, brutally; — paśu-vad-gāmī, vulg. paśu-van-gāmī, adj. Walking like a brute beast
s.f. The fruit of the Spondias mangifera, the hog-plum.

def: s.f. The first stitching or basting (of a garment); running stitch; running and felling.

ety: S. प्र+सूचनीयं; or pasūj˚ = Prk. पसुज्ज˚ = S. प्रसीव्य˚, rt. प्रसिव्
def: v.t. To sew with a running stitch, to run; to baste.
more: v. a. H. sūjī needle. To sew ( sīnā ).

def: s.f. Any small animal.
s.m. See s.v. پشو paśu.
s.f. = paśutva, q.v. s.m. The condition or nature of an animal, bestiality, brutality; the condition of an animal about to be immolated.
s.m. The condition or nature of an animal, bestiality, brutality; the condition of an animal about to be immolated.
wety: Borrowed from Pashto پښتو (paẍto), likely influenced by Persian پشتو (paštu).

def: s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
more: n. m. A leaf ( pattā ). Tum ḍāl ḍāl, to ham pāt pāt . Prov. If you can dart from branch to branch, I can skip from leaf to leaf. (I am stronger than you).

n. m. S. पट to floor. 1. A plank placed across; a board; ledge; platform; stage. 2. The plank on which washermen beat clothes ( dhobī-pāṭ ). 3. The horizontal beam to which the bul locks are attached in a sugar or oil mill, also the place where the driver sits. 4. The width of cloth ( arz ). 5. The breadth of a river ( phāṅt ). 6. A mill stone ( chakkī kā pāṭ ). Maiṅ nājuk. aur chakyā bhārī, Chakyā kā pāṭ mo se nāhīṅ khichego, Mo se pīsno nāhīṅ pisego . Rus. Wom. song. I am so slight, the millstone's such a weight, An inch to move it I'm inadequate. I cannot grind the corn to which I'm set. 7. Silk ( pāṭ-ambar ). 8. A seat; a throne; masnad . 1. Rāj pāṭ kā mohe nahīṅ sāṅsā, Indar padvī kī nahīṅ āsā . Charan Dās. No care have I for royal throne, Nor heavenly dignity to own. 2. Sabhī āë sir nāonā, jahāṅ tumhārā pāṭ . Padmāvat.

s. m. 1. A leaf. 2. An ornament worn in the upper part of the ear. 3. A draught or check on a banker. پاتون آلگنا pātoṅ-ā-lagnā, applied to trees, indicates the fall of their leaves in autumn: metaphorically, the phrase signifies that oneʼs power or ability is exhausted; and, this expression is often applied to patience exhausted by the scorn of a beloved person; or, to a destitute condition from adverse fortune.

def: s.m. Flying, mode of flying, flight; throwing oneself into or down, falling, fall, downfall.
s.m. Leaf (of a tree, book, &c.) draught or cheque on a banker; any thin leaf or plate of metal; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear
subs: v.n. Leaves to fall (in autumn); (met.) to have one's power or endurance exhausted; to be reduced to destitution or misery; to have one's patience tried or exhausted (by the scorn of a beloved person)
s.f. Statement of the assets and liabilities of an estate
pāt-kr̤im or kr̤imi, s.m. lit. 'Leaf-worm'; caterpillar.

ety: Zend pātar; S. pātar?; cf. pād-shāh
def: s.m. Throne.
s.m. = pāṭh, q.v. s.m. Water ( = jal).
more: s. m. 1. Breadth (of cloth or of a river). 2. Tow.
s. m. 1. Silk. 2. A millstone. 3. A throne, seat. 4. A board, shutter, plank, slab, plank on which washermen beat clothes.
s. m. (for पट्ट ) Name of a plant (Corchorus olitorius). ¶ (for पत्र ) Leaf or fold (of a door).

def: s.m. Slab, tablet, board, plank, shutter; fold or leaf (of a door); throne, seat, stool; one of two millstones (either the upper or the lower); the stone or plank on which Indian washermen beat clothes; — bed or plot in a garden or field, terrace; one part, side, or edge of two pieces of cloth; folding clothes or laying them in order; — earthen hoop or brace to prevent a well from falling in; the horizontal beam to which the bullocks are attached in a sugar or oil mill, also the place where the driver sits; sack or load of corn; — silk cloth; cocoon of silk; — the plant Corchorus olitorius (from the fibres of which sacking and cordage are made); tow
subs: (S. paṭṭa+ambara), s.m. Silk cloth; silk garment
pāṭ mahishī, s.f. The queen who is installed or consecrated with the king, principal wife of a rājā, queen-consort

def: s.f. Good name, honour, character, reputation, credit (syn. ābrū)
more: n. f. 1. S. पत्यारा Credit; honor. See آبرو 1. Mukh merā chūmat din rāt, Hoṭan lagat kahat na bāt, Jā se merī jag meṅ pat, "Esakhī, sājan?" Nā, sakhī, Nath ." Riddle. Mukrī . My mouth he kisses night and day, Sticks to my lips, has nought to say, To him my honor still doth cling. 'A lover, dear?' 'No, dear, nose-ring.' 2. Duarā baiṭhe samdhī, dahī-jarā, saiyāṅ ke baṛī Morā ghare nunavoṅ nāhīṅ, tu patī rākhahu, Rām! Bhoj. Song. Samdhi —his daughter wrong—sits at the door, my husband's name is great, No salt there's in the house, O Ram, my honor keep inviolate! 2. Lord; husband; master. pat utārnā , v. a. To disgrace ( ābrū utārnā ). pat jānā , v. n. To be discredited, dishonored, disgraced. pat rakhnā , v. a. To preserve one's good name, credit, honor. 1. Rakh pat, rakhā pat . Prov. Honor pay, honor get. (As thou givest so shall thou receive). 2. ' Har Har' kare Daropdī, 'nagan karo mat m…

n. m. S. पटत्. 1. A sound of falling, breaking, or beating. 2. A willing catamite ( auṅdhā ). paṭ , adv. At once; instantly; there and then. Chaṭ maṅgnī, paṭ biyāh . Prov. No sooner asked than wed. paṭ paṭ, paṭā paṭ , adv. Continuously; without stopping.

adj. 1. On the belly (opp. of chit . q. v.). 2. Upside down; upset; overturned; inverted. Chit bhī merī, paṭ bhī merī . Prov. Heads I win, tails too I win. (Might is right). 3. Useless; ineffective ( birthā ). Saif to paṭ paṛī thī, par nīmche ne kām kīyā . The dagger execution did the sword fell flat. (The liberality of the son shames the parsimony of the father). 4. Fallow; uncultivated; uninhabited (a village). 5. Forgotten; obliterated. Sab likhā paṛhā paṭ ho gayā .
n. m. S. पट्ट throne. A royal grant engraved on copper, stone, etc.

n. m. S. कपाट from पट् to surround. 1. A shutter; leaf of a door; the valve of a folding door ( kivāṛ ). 2. A screen; covering; veil. Āṅkh, nāk, mukh mūṅd-ke, nām Niranjan le, Bhītar ke paṭ jab khuleṅ, jab bāhar ke paṭ de . Dohā. Your eyes and nose and mouth all close before God's name you take, When all the outer doors are shut your mental eye will wake. paṭ bheṛnā, band karnā, uḍhālnā, uṛaknā, denā, lagānā, yā mārnā , v. n. To shut or close the door. paṭ kholnā , v. n. 1. To open the door. 2. To take off or lift a covering or veil; remove a screen. Kab ke gaë, kahāṅ rain gaṅvāī, mat ghūṅghaṭ paṭ kholo jī . Wom. Song. Ṭhumrī .
1. ( पद ) s. f. Good name, honour, character. 2. ( पति ) s. m. A lord, master, husband. 3. pati ( पत्र ) s. m. A leaf.
subs: (-kī), To drag down (one's) good name, &c., to ruin the character, &c. (of), to disgrace
v.n. To lose one's good name, &c.; to be discredited, dishonoured, or disgraced
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: adj. Lying flat, lying on the face, upside down, overturned, upset; prone, inclined; falling flat, useless, ineffective (as the blow of a sword, &c.); lying uncultivated, fallow, waste (land); uninhabited, deserted (village); lying neglected, forgotten, effaced, wiped out; — s.m. A willing catamite
more: 1. s. m. A shutter, the valve of a folding door. 2. adj. Upside down, overturned.
s. m. The sound of falling or beating.
s. m. A menstruum, solvent, flux.
subs: (paṭ+par, or paṭ+paṛ, rt. of paṛnā), adj. & s.m. Level, flat; bare of trees, &c.; a bare plain, a desert waste; moist land caked by sunshine after rain or a flood
s.m. A flat surface; a bare plain, &c. ( = paṭpaṛ); flatness, evenness

def: s.m. Sound of falling, or of breaking or of beating; — adv. Quickly, instantly, at once, in a trice
subs: paṭ-paṭ (S. paṭa-paṭa), s.m. Continuous sound of beating, or of falling rain, &c.; — adv. Continuously, without ceasing
s.m. A beating; — paṭ-paṭ denā, v.t. To give (a child) a beating.

def: s.m. Cloth, coarse cloth or canvas; piece of cloth; fold of cloth; covering, screen, veil; a chequered cloth or board (on which chess, &c. is played); chair, throne; turban, tiara (in this sense it occurs in comp., as paṭ-rānī); a royal grant engraved on copper or stone, &c.
subs: paṭ-rānī, s.f. A queen (decorated with the tiara), the principal wife of a rājā or king ( = pāṭ-rānī)
(-kā), To open, or to remove, a screen; to take off or remove a covering; to lift, or to remove, a veil
s.m. The kind of cloth called paṭ.

def: s.m. Leaf of a door; valve of a folding door; door, shutter
subs: paṭ uṛaknā, paṭ band karnā, paṭ bheṛnā, paṭ lagānā, paṭ mārnā, v.n. To close a door

def: s.m. Breadth, expanse, extension; width or breadth (of cloth, of a river, &c.); (in Geom.) the intersection of a produced side of a figure and a perpendicular on it, or the figure formed by such intersection.
s.m. A leaf (used chiefly as first member of comp.)
subs: pat-jhaṛ, adj. & s.f. With the leaves dropped off, leafless; — fall of the leaf, autumn; — pat-jhaṛ honā, v.n. To lose its leaves in autumn (a tree); (met.) to decay (said of an aged person)
s.f. The fall of the leaf, autumn.
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: s.m. Leaf = pāt; pattā; q.v. s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
adj. & s.m. = pātr̤i, q.v.
part. adj. Watched, protected, preserved.
s.m. Plank, board, bench; slab or plank on which washermen beat clothes ( = pāṭ).
more: s. m. A plank on which washermen beat clothes.

def: s.m. Sign, mark, signal, symptom, token, trace, clue, hint, direction, specification, particular mention; address (of a person), address (or place to which one is directed), label; book of instructions and regulations (for rent-collectors, &c.)
more: n. m. S. पत्ता sign. 1. Sign; symptom; specification; particular mention. See اشاره (2, 5, 6). 2. The address of a person; the address or place to which one is directed. patā denā , v. a. To direct; point; give a clue. pate kī kahnā , v. n. To expose one. Maiṅ bhī phir pate kī kahūṅgā. patā lagānā, yā pānā, surāg lagānā , v. n. 1. To trace; track; follow up a clue. 2. To search; search out; seek for; cast about for. be-pate kī chiṭṭhī , adj. An undirected letter; a dead letter.

n. f. A straight, long, and broad sword with two edges ( du-dhārā ); a foil or wooden sword used in fencing. paṭā-bāz, paṭait , n. m. A fencer; one who plays with clubs. paṭā-bāzī , n. f. Fencing; playing with clubs.
s. m. A sign, mark, symptom, hint, token, direction, address or place to which one is directed, label.
subs: (-kā), To obtain a clue (to), to trace, &c. = patā lagānā, q.v.
(-kā), To give the clue, or direction, or address (of); — to direct, point out
(-kā), To trace out, find out, or discover (the personality, abode, business, &c. of a person); to follow up a clue; to search, search out.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. A straight, long, and broad sword with two edges; a wooden sword or foil (used in fencing, &c.), a cudgel; a board on which Hindūs sit whilst eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies ( = paṭṭā); a streak, stripe, broad line; a kind of bracelet; half the beard or one side of the face; a leaf of the American aloe or of any similar plant; the head-stall of a bridle; a long narrow slip or strip of coarse cloth, matting, &c.
more: s. m. 1. ( पट्टिश ) A foil, a wooden scimitar for cudgelling with. پٿي باز paṭe-bāz, (1.) A fencer, a cudgeller. (2.) ( met. ) A coquette, a wanton, a strumpet. پٿاکاٿنا paṭā kāṭnā, v. a. To discharge from service. ¶ 2. ( पट्ट ) A board on which Hindūs sit whilst eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies.
subs: s.m. One who plays with foils or cudgels, a fencer; a man who fights with sword and shield; — (met.), coquette, wanton, strumpet
s.f. Playing with foils or cudgels, fencing
v.n. To practice with foils, to fence.

def: n. m. A sock; stocking.
s.m. Winged or flying animal; a bird.
s.f. = paṭah, q.v.
s.m. Drum, kettle-drum.
s.f. A small shrub, Abrus precatorius.
s.m. Straw or grass spread over a field to protect the fresh-planted roots from the sun.
s.f. Collection of leaves (esp. of the sugar-cane).
s.m. Lease-holder; a village priest.
s.m. One who practises with foils, &c.; fencer; cudgel-player; — a lout, a blockhead.
s.m. That which causes to fall or sink; sin, crime, guilt; — uncleanness, impurity, defilement, pollution
more: n. m. S. पातुक A large aquatic animal; a porpoise; ( fig .) the water elephant.
n. m. H. pātī , a letter. A messenger; letter-bearer.

n. m. S. पत् to fall. 1. That which causes to fall or sink; sin ( pāp ); wickedness. Gur-sevā sab bighan bināse; Dur-mat bhāje, pātak nāse . Charan Dās. The worship of your priest your sin will take away, Your evil thoughts expel, your tendency to stray. 2. Pollution from proximity to a corpse. pātak lagnā , v. n. 1. To be polluted. 2. To be stained, sullied (one's character); to be stigmatised. panch pātak , n. m. The five most heinous sins of the Brahmanical code; viz. killing a Brahman, stealing, drinking spirits, intercourse with the wife of a spiritual preceptor, and association with one who has committed these sins.
subs: v.n. To be touched with anything unclean, be defiled or polluted; to be stained or sullied (the character).

def: s.m. Letter-bearer, messenger.
s.m. A loud sound, a bursting sound, a crash
more: s. m. A sound, a crash.
subs: adv. With a crash, loud, loudly.

def: s.m. Throwing, dashing down; throw, dash, fall (see paṭaknā and paṭkan).
more: s. A dash ( Harris ): see پٿکنا paṭaknā.

def: s.m. Cotton cloth; camp, encampment.
s.m. = S پتاکا पताका patākā, s.f. Flag, banner; flag-staff; emblem carried as an ensign or banner, symbol, sign, mark; a particular high number; an episode in a drama.
more: n. f. S. पठ् to fall. A loud sound; a crash. Woh phir paṭākh se bol uṭṭhā .

def: s.f. Flag, banner.
s.f. = S پتاك पताक patāk, s.m. Flag, banner; flag-staff; emblem carried as an ensign or banner, symbol, sign, mark; a particular high number; an episode in a drama.
more: n. m. S. पताका Pr. paḍāā . A flag; banner.
n. m. 1. A cracker; a squib; the cap of a gun. 2. (Slang) . A beautiful or graceful (woman or boy); a belle.
s. f. A standard, a flag.

def: s.m. Cracker; squib; a gun-cap.
s.m. = paṭākā, q.v. s.f. = S پتاك पताक patāk, s.m. Flag, banner; flag-staff; emblem carried as an ensign or banner, symbol, sign, mark; a particular high number; an episode in a drama.
more: (properly, پٿاکا from پٿاک A sound) s. m. A cracker, a squib.
wety: Hyperforeign form of پَٹاکا (paṭākā). Displaced پَٹاکا (paṭākā). Onomatopoeic, from پٹ (paṭ, “crack”).

def: s.m. Cracker; squib; a gun-cap.

v.t. and n. = paṭaknā, q.v. v.t. To dash against, to knock down, dash down, to throw (one) down with violence; to throw about (the arms and legs) convulsively; — v.n. To abate, go down, or subside (a swelling or tumour); to crack, crackle (see paṭ)
adj. & s.m. Fallen, sinful, guilty, criminal; — sinner, criminal.
s.m. Ensign, standard-bearer; one who gives a signal.

ety: S. पात, rt. पत्+अक+अनीयं; see paṛnā
def: v.t. To dash against, to knock down, dash down, to throw (one) down with violence; to throw about (the arms and legs) convulsively; — v.n. To abate, go down, or subside (a swelling or tumour); to crack, crackle (see paṭ)
more: (r. पट Move) 1. v. a. To dash against any thing, to throw on the ground with violence, to knock. 2. v. n. To crackle, to crack.

def: v. a. S. पत to fall. 1. To dash against; to throw on the ground with violence. 2. To crack. 3. To sink or go down (a swelling).
more: n. m. A violent throw or fall (in wrestling) ( bhadkanā ). paṭkanā denā, yā sunānā , v. a. To dash one on the ground with violence (in wrestling). Aisā paṭkanā sanāyā kī chāroṅ khāne chit āyā. paṭkanā khānā; , Rus. paṭok khānā , v. n. To be dashed to, or to fall on the ground with force.

def: s.m. Letter-bearer, messenger.

s.m. One of the seven regions under the earth and the abode of the Nāgas or serpents and demons; hell, the infernal regions; — hole, chasm, excavation; submarine fire; — a sort of apparatus for calcining and subliming metals (formed of two earthen pots, the upper one inverted over the lower, and the two joined together by their necks with cement and placed in a hole containing fire)
more: n. f. 1. Regions under the earth, or towards the south pole, inhabited by a serpentine race; lower regions; infernal regions ( patāl lok ); hell. 2. Nadir. Man-chītī hotī nahīṅ Pirbhū chītī tat-kāl, Bal chāhat the Suarg ko, bhej dīye pātāl . Dohā. Man's will availeth not, God's will is instant done, Bal wished to go to Heaven, to Hell he was sent down. 3. An apparatus for calcining and subliming metals consisting of two earthen pots, the upper one inverted over the lower, and the two joined together by their necks with cement and placed in a hole containing fire.
s. m. Regions, according to the Hindūs, under the earth or towards the south-pole, inhabited by a serpentine race; infernal regions; hell.
subs: s.f. The Ganges of the infernal regions.

def: adj. & s.m. Pale-red, of a pink or rose colour; — pale-red hue, pink, rose-colour, redness; — the trumpet flower, Bignonia suaveolens; — a species of rice that ripens in the rains

s.m. Roof, thatch; cover, covering, veil; a film over the eye; a basket; heap, mass, number, quantity, multitude; retinue, train; book, division, section, chapter (of a book); a formulary (of ritual) worship; a sandal-wood mark on the forehead.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit पटल (paṭala).

def: s.f. The trumpet flower, Bignonia suaveolens.
s. (dakh.) Battalion ( Harris ).
s. (dakh.) Battalion.
s.f. = pāṭalā, q.v.; — name of a city
subs: s.m. Name of the capital of Magadh (near the confluence of the Son and Ganges), supposed to be the ancient Palibothra and the modern Patna.

def: adj. Closed; shut (the eyes). paṭam honā, paṭ ho jānā , v. n. To be blind, closed (the eyes). Yā Elāhī! jo jhūṭī qasmeṅ khāëṅ, Dono āṅkheṅ abhī paṭam ho jāëṅ! Wom. Shauq.
adj. Closed, shut (the eyes)
subs: v.n. To be closed (the eyes), to be blind ( = paṭ ho-jānā).

def: s.m. See s.v. pāṭ. s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
more: s. m. Silk cloth, a silk garment.

def: s.m. = pāṭ-ambar, q.v. s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
s.f. Shoe, slipper.
more: Rus. n. f. H. pāṅoṅ a foot. A shoe; a slipper. Pī kī pātan sir dharo, dharo charan par sīs, Bāsā ho baikuṅṭh māṅ phir to biswe bīs . Rus. Dohā. My husband's shoes upon my head, my head laid on his feet, For th' Heavenly courts above I'll surely be a dweller meet.

def: s.m. (Braj) pl. of pāṭ, 'slab' &c., q.v.
more: 1. s. f. A roof. 2. s. m. Name of a city.

def: s.f. Roofing; roof, &c. (see pāṭnā); — city, mart; name of the city of Patna.
s.m. Falling; fall; falling from dignity, virtue, &c.; hanging down, becoming slack; death.
more: s. m. The act of falling. पत्तन pattan (in comp.) A town, a city.

def: s.m. Roofing, &c. (see pāṭan and paṭnā).
v. a. 1. To realize cash. Huṅḍī paṭā lāo . 2. To irrigate; water (a field). E. Khet meṅ pānī paṭā do . 3. To cause to be boarded, roofed, covered. 4. To settle; conclude (a bargain).

ety: caus. of paṭnā, q.v.; and see also pāṭnā
def: v.t. To cause to be boarded, roofed, or covered; to place the beams on the roof of a house; to board (a room); to water, irrigate (a field); to prepare a ćaukā with cowdung or earth dissolved in water; to fill up, make level (a hole, &c.); to realize the amount (of a bill, &c.); to pay money, settle (an account), conclude (a bargain).
more: (trans. of پٿنا) v. a. 1. To irrigate, to water. 2. To prepare a چوکا chaukā with cow-dung dissolved in water. 3. To place the beams on the roof of a house. 4. To pay money.

def: s.m. Sappan wood, &c. = patrāng, q.v.s.v. patra.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पतङ्ग (pataṅga, “the flier, flying insect e.g. moth”).

def: To fly a paper kite.
s.m. A bird; a flying insect, grasshopper, moth; a paper kite (syn. kankauwā); the sun; a ball for playing with (syn. geṅd); a name of Kr̤ishṇa
more: n. m. S. पतङ्ग 1. A moth ( parvānah ). 2. A paper kite ( kankavvā ). 3. Sapan-wood. pataṅg uṛā'nā , v. a. To fly a kite. pataṅg chhurī , Wom. adj. A firebrand ( āg-lagāū ). pataṅg-bāzī , n. f. Kite-flying.
s. m. 1. A moth. 2. A paper kite. 3. The sun.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पतङ्ग (pataṅga, “the flier, flying insect e.g. moth”).

def: s.m. A flying insect; moth; grasshopper; a spark (of fire), a live coal
more: n. m. 1. A spark; live coal. 2. A worm.
s. m. A spark. پتنگاهونا patangā honā, To go rapidly about a business.
subs: v.n. To move about briskly, go rapidly about a business.

def: s.f. Name of one of the wives of Tārksha, and mother of the patagas or flying animals.
s.f. A small bird; a sort of bee.
adj. & s.m. Composed by Patanjali; — the Yoga system of philosophy (first taught by Patanjali).
more: n. m. The Yoga system of philosophy.

def: s.m. Name of a saint or muni, teacher of the Yoga philosophy (thence called pātanjala); name of the celebrated author of the Mahā-bhāshya or great commentary on Pāṇini.
n. m. 1. Irrigation. 2. Roofing a house with tiles, etc. 3. Beams; rafters, or stones, etc. for roofing. Qabr ke līye paṭāo nahīṅ hai .
s.m. Roofing (a house with tiles, &c.); covering (a floor, &c.) with boards; apparatus for roofing or covering (such as) beams, rafters, planks, &c.; watering, or flooding (a field), irrigation.
more: (from پٿانا) s. m. 1. Irrigating, flooding a field. 2. Roofing a house with tiles, &c.

def: s.m. A kind of fruit.
more: s. m. Name of a fruit.

def: n. m. 1. The sound of beating or of the falling of drops of rain. 2. The prattle of children.
s.m. see s.v. paṭ, 'Sound of beating.' s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
more: s. m. The sound of beating or of the falling of drops of rain.

def: s.m. Moth, butterfly.
s.f. Prostitute; dancing-girl
more: E. n. f. A dancing girl. Hin. See (بيسوا 1). Chātar kā kām nahīṅ pātar se aṭke, Pātar kā kām yehī liyā dīyā saṭke . Prov. 'Tis not a wise man's part to be by harlot caught, The harlot gives and takes—and off she's like a shot.

n. m. A receptacle; cup; jar, etc. (برتن). Ādh ser ke pātr meṅ kaise ser samāe? Prov. A half-seer measure cannot hold a seer. pātr, pātar , adj. Fit; worthy; eligible. jal-pātr , n. m. A drinking vessel ( loṭā ). su-pātr , adj. Good; virtuous; meritorious. Yeh laṛkā baṛā su-pātr uṭṭhā hai. ku-pātr , n. m. A bad man; a sinner. Ku-pātr ko dān na dīje .
s. m. A vessel; adj. Capable, worthy, able, eligible, fit.
1. s. f. A prostitute, a dancing girl. 2. adj. Weak, lean.

def: adj. Spare, emaciate, thin, lean; weak ( = patlā).
n. See H. پرستهان Safar ke chalne kā jab dil ne iztirāb kīyā, Nikal-ke āṅkh se āṅsū ne pā-tarāb kīyā . Fasāna ajāib.
s.f. = pātar, pātariyā, qq.v. s.f. Prostitute; dancing-girl
more: s. f. A prostitute. پترياباز patariyā-bāz, A whoremonger.

def: s.f. lit. 'Winged'; a bird; an arrow.
s.m. = pāṭaunī; paṭnī; qq.v.
more: s. m. A ferryman. See پاٿوي.

def: s.m. A leaf.
more: n. m. S. पट् to string. 1. One who strings beads, pearls, etc. or makes braid, fringe, tape, etc. ( ilāqe-band ). 2. Tow made from the Hybiscus cannabinus ; sorrel ( kudram ); a potherb.

def: adj. & s.m. Clever, sharp, dexterous; — sharpness, acuteness, intensity, energy; cleverness, skill, dexterity, talent; quickness, precipitation.
adj. Clever, adroit, dexterous; cunning, crafty, fraudulent.
s. (dakh.) Bravery, valour (?).
s.m. Mortgage; pledge; a pledge of which the usufruct pays both the principal and interest within a definite period (and thus ensures its own redemption).
n. m. A living pledge; mortgage; a pledge the usufruct of which pays both the principal and interest within a certain period, and therefore ensures its own redemption.
s.m. A fire-fly (syn. jugnū; shab-tāb).
adv. Each day, always, constantly.
s.m. Fencing with the paṭā; cudgelling
more: s. m. 1. Cudgelling. 2. Sovereignty. 3. A chief among the Kunbī tribe. 4. A title of Mahārāttas, the head man of a village. پٿيل ڐالنا paṭel ḍālnā, 1. To interrupt, to throw obstacles in the way of a business. 2. To beat.
subs: (-meṅ, or -par), To interrupt, to throw obstacles (in the way of); to cudgel, to beat.

def: s.m. Sovereignty; a chief among the Kumbī tribe; a Kumbī; the headman of a village, a title of Marhaṭṭās.

s.m. = paṭerā, q.v. s.m. The plant Cyperus hexastachyus communis; the reed or flag Cyperus papyrus; a long coarse grass (used as fodder for elephants, and made into mats, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poorer classes).
s.m. A stone, slab, slate; a log or plank used as a harrow; a large flat-bottomed boat.
more: s. m. 1. A kind of boat, flat-bottomed. 2. A log or beam used to roll or harrow ground.

def: s.f. A small flat-bottomed boat.
more: (dimin. of پٿيلا) s. f. A small flatbottomed boat.

def: s.m. = paṭīnā, q.v. s.m. A species of bird, the green fly-catcher, Merops viridis.

s.m. The plant Cyperus hexastachyus communis; the reed or flag Cyperus papyrus; a long coarse grass (used as fodder for elephants, and made into mats, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poorer classes).

s.m. The plant Cyperus hexastachyus communis; the reed or flag Cyperus papyrus; a long coarse grass (used as fodder for elephants, and made into mats, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poorer classes).
s.m. Water ( = jal).
s.m. Path, course, way, road, highway
subs: s.m. Travelling expenses; provisions for a journey
s.m. 'Way-showing, or shower'; guide, leader, director, conductor
s.m. One who guards a way.

def: s.m. Proper, fit, or wholesome food (for a patient), diet, regimen; spoon-diet, broth, soup; — syrup, sherbet (esp. as a regimen).
s.m. A young full-grown animal; a youth in his prime, a well-developed youth; a wrestler; a young tree which has not yet borne fruit ( = paṭṭhā; pāṭhā).
s. m. A reading, lecture, perusal (of a book), lesson.
n. m. S. पथ्य 1. Spoon-diet. Kāhū ko baiṅgan biyāle aur kāhū ko baiṅgan pachch . Prov. Some brinjal maketh ill, some it digest at will. (Constitutions differ). 2. Syrup; sherbet.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पथ (pathá), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *patʰás, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *patʰás, from Proto-Indo-European *pent- (“path”).

def: n. m. S. पठ् to read. 1. A lesson; task. 2. A prayer or portion of the scripture ( vazīfā ). pāṭh karnā , Hin. v. n. 1. To repeat or revise a lesson. 2. To recite or repeat daily prayers ( vazīfa paṛhnā . Mah.).
s.m. Reading, perusal, study, recital (esp. sacred study); text of reading; lecture; lesson, task
subs: s.f. Place where the Vedas are read or studied
pāṭh karnā, v.n. To read, study; to repeat or revise a lesson; to recite or repeat daily portions of the Vedas, &c.
pāṭh-sālā, s.f. Reading-room, lecture-hall, school-house, college, primary (Hindī) school
pāṭh manjarī, s.f. A particular small bird, Graculus religiosa
s.m. Reading-master; professor of elocution; preceptor, teacher, schoolmaster

def: s.m. Young, full-grown animal (commonly a goat, deer, or an elephant; syn. paṭh, paṭṭhā); a robust youth; a young wrestler.
more: s. m. A young animal full grown (generally, a goat or elephant), a youth or young wrestler.

def: n. m. 1. A full grown animal (generally an elephant). 2. A robust young man; a wrestler. Sāṭhā pāṭhā, bīsī khīsī . Prov. At sixty a man's like an elephant strong, a woman at twenty is on the wane.
part. = paṭhāyā, perf. part. of paṭhānā.

s.m. Reader, lecturer, preceptor, tutor, teacher, professor; spiritual preceptor; a Brāhman who reads in public the Purāṇas or other sacred works of the Hindūs; a panḍit who declares what is the law or custom according to the sacred writings; a title of Brāhmans.
more: s. m. 1. He that gives lessons, a teacher, a professor. 2. A title of Brāhmaṇs.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पाठक (pāṭhaka).

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), Reader, reciter.
n. m. 1. A teacher; tutor; professor. 2. A Brahman who reads religious epic poems and the Purans in public. 3. A caste or title of Brahmans.
s.m. Bdellium.
s.m. Reading; lecturing, teaching.

s.m. Name of a race inhabiting the hilly country to the north-west of Lahore (syn. Afg̠ān); name of a race or dynasty that reigned in Dehli; name of a tribe of Mohammadans (descended from the paṭhān or Afg̠hān race, who settled in India, particularly in Ruhelkhand); soldier, warrior, man-at-arms.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀧𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀸𑀡 (*paṭṭhāṇa), from Proto-Middle Indo-Aryan *𑀧𑀱𑁆𑀝𑀸𑀦 (*paṣṭāna), borrowed from Proto-Iranian. Compare Punjabi پَٹھاݨ (paṭhāṇ), Odia ପଠାଣ (paṭhāṇa), Gujarati પઠાણ (paṭhāṇ), Marathi पठाण (paṭhāṇ), Bengali পাঠান (paṭhan). Related to Pashto پښتانۀ (paẍtânë); see there for more.

def: s.m. Reading, reciting; mentioning; reader, student.

n. m. paṭhānī , n. f. 1. A people, called also Afgāns, inhabiting the hilly country to the North-West of Lahore. They derive their origin, and name from a grandson of Malik Tālūt or King Saul . 2. A soldier; warrior; man-at-arms. 1. Kheṅcho na kamān, bana na Paṭhān! Prov. 2. Tīr, na kamān, kāhe ke Paṭhān ? Prov. Nor bow nor arrow, and yet a Pathān!

ety: paṭhā˚ = paṭhāw˚ = Prk. पट्ठावे(इ), or पट्ठाव(इ) = S. प्रस्थापय(ति), caus. of rt. प्र+स्था
def: v.t. To send, despatch ( = bhejnā).

E. v. a. S. प्रस्थापन To send. See bhejnā. Nandī, dekho balam kī batyāṅ! Ap āveṅ na paṭheveṅ patyāṅ! Wom. Song. My sister-in-law! my husband's conduct only see! He neither comes himself, nor even writes to me!
s.f. A woman of the paṭhān race; the wife of a paṭhān.
oblique plural of پَٹھان (paṭhān, “Pashtun”)
vocative plural of پَٹھان (paṭhān, “Pashtun”)
v.t. = paṭhānā, q.v. v.t. To send, despatch ( = bhejnā).

s.m. Stone = patthar, q.v. s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.
more: s. m. A stone. See پتهر patthar.
subs: s.m. One who plays at stone-throwing; one who is fond of, or is addicted to, throwiug stones; — patthar-bāzī, s.f. Stone-throwing; fondness for stone-throwing
v.n. The melting of a stony heart; a difficult task to be facilitated
(-par), Stones to fall (upon); to hail; to be crushed under a shower of stones or hailstones; to be overwhelmed with trouble, &c.; to be injured, ruined or destroyed
v.n. A stony heart to be softened or melted
s.m. A breaker of stones; — the woodpecker, Picus
(-par), To throw stones (at), to pelt with stones
patthar-tale hāth ānā or dabnā = patthar tale dāman dabnā
v.n. To be entangled in difficulties
s.m. One who is attached to the very stones of his house and who clings to it even in distress; a skinflint; a kind of serpent; a sort of fish; a sort of pot-herb
v.t. To put (a knife, &c.), to the grindstone or hone; to whet or sharpen (a knife, &c.)
(or ćhātī par patthar rakhnā), v.n. To be patient by compulsion, or from being without remedy, to suffer patiently
v.n. lit. 'To carry stones'; to labour hard
v.t. To give a rude or sharp answer; to reply rashly without understanding the meaning of one's question; to give a blunt refusal
v.t. lit. 'To break one's own head with a stone'; to teach or instruct a fool
s.m. Stony or hard heart; hard-heartedness
adj. lit. 'A line engraved on stone'; ineffaceable, ineradicable, unalterable

v.n. To become stone, to be petrified ( = pathrānā); to become hard as stone, to become rigid; to be heavy or burdensome; to be unmerciful; to receive no impression, to be impervious (to); to be fixed or immovable, to be still as a statue, be stable, stand still; to freeze, to congeal; to be stone-blind; to become useless or unserviceable.

def: s. m. See پتهر Stone.

n. m. A firelock. patthar tale hāth ānā, ya dabnā, patthar tale dāman dabnā , v. n. To be entangled in difficulties. patthar kī lakīr , adj. lit . A line engraved on stone. Ineffaceable; ineradicable; unalterable. 1. Unkī bāt patthar kī lakīr hai . Prov. Saying. His word is as ineffaceable as though it were engraven on stone. 2. Razīloṅ kī dostī pānī kī lakīr, Sharīfoṅ kī dostī patthar kī lakīr . Prov. The friendship of the base a line on water traced, The firmness of high birth a line on stone engraved.
s.m. Water ( = pāth)
subs: pātho-nidhi, s.m. lit. 'Receptacle, or treasure-house, of waters'; the ocean.

def: v. a. To send.
n. m. A brick maker.
s.m. Provender or provisions for a journey, viaticum.

adj. & s.m. Knowing, conversant with; — a Brāhman who has finished his studies; one who has read or studied (any subject), student; one who gives lessons, teacher, professor; — the plant Plumbago zeylanica; — a kind of fish.
more: 1. s. m. The same as پاٿهک pāṭhak, q. v. 2. s. f. A young goat.

def: s.m. The tree Acacia catechu; or Mimosa alba.

adj. Unfrequented, untravelled, deserted (as a road, or a caravansary).

def: s.f. Female student; female lecturer.
s.m. = pāṭhak, q.v.; — a kind of sheat-fish, Silurus pelorius or Boalis.

s. (dakh.) See پناه panāh.

def: s.m. Courier or messenger sent from place to place.

ety: S. स्थापनीयं per met.; cf. H. thāpnā
def: v.t. To make up cowdung into cakes for fuel.
more: v. a. To make cakes of cow-dung for fuel. 1. Āj ke pāthe āj hī nahīṅ jalte . Prov. The cow-pats made to-day wont burn to-day. 2. Kākī uple pāthe nāhīṅ, mo se nit pathwāve saë . W. Rus. Wom. Song. No cow-dung pats my aunt e'er makes, but always makes me make the cakes.
(perhaps, from पत्र A leaf, &c.) v. a. To make up cow-dung into cakes for fuel.

def: v.t. To read, &c. = paṛhnā, q.v. v.t. To read, repeat, recite; to say; to mutter a spell; to learn, study (with acc. & abl.); to make out, decipher; to talk (as a bird)
subs: adj. Fit to be read; what can be read, readable, legible
paṛhne-hārā, s.m. (f. -ī), Reader, reciter; learner, student.

def: v.n. To be made into cakes (cowdung).
more: v. n. To be made into cakes (cowdung).

def: n. A chicken; a kid; a lamb.
s.m. Young (of an animal), kid; lamb; chicken, &c.

s.m. = paṭrā, q.v. s.m. & adj. Vessel (in general), goblet, bowl, cup, plate, dish, jar, pot; utensil; receptacle, recipient, repository, proper object; what holds or supports; king's counsellor or minister; the bed of a river; a leaf; — capable, worthy, fit, eligible, deserving, able; accomplished (used in comp.).
v.n. To be petrified; to become hard, to indurate, &c. ( = patthar honā, q.v.s.v. patthar).
more: v. n. 1. To be pe/?/d. 2. To become hard (a boil); to indurate. Rāh dekhte dekhte āṅkheṅ pathrā gaīṅ. pathrāo . Stoning; the act of stoning. patthrā chauth , n. f. The 4th day of the 2nd fortnight in Bhādon. It is considered a bad omen to see the moon on this day if one has not seen her on the second day of this fortnight, and, to cast off its influence, the unlucky person throws stones into his neighbour's house. The abuse he receives in return wards off the evil effects. Hence the name pathra-chauth. patthar barasnā , v. n. To hail. patthar paṛnā , v. n. 1. To fall (stones); to be crushed under stones, or hailstones. Jā, jal-gaë, tujh pe patthar paṛeṅ! Hin. Wom. Away! fire burn you! may stones fall on you. 2. To be damaged, ruined; destroyed. 3. (Ironic.) To make a grand display ( āg lagānā , (5), used by Women. patthar-phoṛ , n. m. One who breaks stones on the road. patthar pheknā , v…
v. n. 1. To be petrified. 2. To become hard (a bile), to indurate. آنکهـ پتهراني See under آنکهـ.

To be fixed or immovable; to be still as a statue. 4. To congeal; freeze. 5. To be stone-blind. 6. To become useless or unserviceable.

adj. & s.m. Belonging to a road; furthering, helping; agreeing with, proper, fit, suitable, wholesome (used in a medical sense of diet, regimen, &c); — wholesome diet, &c. = path, q.v.

n. f. See paṭṭhā (4, 5) A young lady; lass; damsel. 1. Bārah baras kī paṭhyā, bīs baras kī ṭaṭyā . Prov. At twelve years old a Bantam Cock, at twenty but an empty smock. 2. Meri sās hai sāṭh baras kī, Apne man se rahat paṭhyā! Wom. Rus. song. My mother-in-law is in her sixtieth year, In her own mind she's still a lusty steer!
s.m. = pati; and pāṅti; qq.v.
s.f. Leaf; leaf on which anything has been written, letter, note, epistle, document, &c.; sign, trace, clue ( = patā).
more: n. f. S. प्रवृत्ति, , Pr. pauttī news, S. पत्री a letter. 1. A letter; note ( chiṭṭhī ); message. Chīr phāṛ patyā karūṅ, uṅglī qalam dawāt, Nain poṅchh siyāhī karūṅ, likh detī do bāt . Dohā. For paper I will tear my wrap, my finger be my pen, My eyes' lamp-black shall serve for ink, two lines my love I'll pen. 2. Kanak-patr kāgad bhayo, mas bhayo mānak mol, Kaun gunāh ham ne kīyo, likh na sakī do bol? (From the husband to his two wives). Is paper grown as dear as gold, or ink as jewels sold, What's my offence that not a word to me you've writ or told. Kar kampe, likhnī ṭare, aṅg aṅg tharrāë, Sudh āve, chhāti phaṭe, pātī likhī na jāë . The 1st wife's reply. My hand shakes so, my pen doth slip, my body quakes all o'er, Your image thinking on my full heart bursts—I cant write more. Pātī to wā ko likhūṅ jo koī hoë bides, Tan meṅ, man meṅ bas rahyo, tāko kahā saṅdes? The 2nd wife's reply. A l…

n. f. S. पाट्टिका 1. A side of a bedstead ( bāī ). Khasam kīyā sukh sone ko, ki pāṭī lag-ke rone ko? E. Prov. Have I a husband wed to enjoy my bed, or weep on it instead? 2. A smooth cool mat ( sītal pāṭī ).
s. f. 1. ( पत्री ) A letter, note, epistle. 2. ( पत्र ) A leaf.

def: s.f. Side-pieces of a bedstead; a kind of board on which children learn to write; a kind of mat ( = sītal pāṭi); parting of the hair in the middle of the head; a kind of sweetmeat.
more: s. f. 1. The side pieces of a bedstead. 2. A kind of mat. 3. A kind of board on which children learn to write. 4. Divisions of the hair which is combed towards the two sides and parted by a line in the middle. 5. A kind of sweetmeat.

more: n. m. S. पति from पा to protect. Owner; master; lord; husband. pati-birt , n. m. Fidelity to a husband; chastity. pati-birtā, pati-bhartā , n. f. S. पतिव्रता A faithful, virtuous, or devoted wife.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pattiā), from Sanskrit पत्त्रिका (pattrikā), from पत्त्र (pattra) + -इका (-ikā).

pati-vantī, pati-vatnī, s.f. A woman who possesses a husband, a married woman, a wife whose husband is living
vulg. pati-brat, s.m. Loyalty or fidelity to a husband; chastity, modesty
vulg. pati-bratā, pati-birtā, pati-bhartā, s.f. Faithful, virtuous, and devoted wife
wety: From Sanskrit पित्त (pitta).

def: s.m. See s.v. pati.
adj. f. Chaste (woman).
adv. = prati-din, q.v. adv. From day to day, day by day, every day, daily; always, continually.

s.m. = prati-grah, q.v. s.m. Receiving, accepting, acceptance, receiving a donation; one who receives, receiver; a gift, present; a proper donation or fit present to a Brāhman at suitable periods; the right of accepting gifts (as the peculiar prerogative of Brāhmans); friendly reception; the reserve of an army; a spitting-pot, spittoon; the sun near the moon's node.
s.m. The Gangetic porpoise, Delphinus gangeticus (syn. sūṅs).
adj. Thin, &c. = patlā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Spare, emaciate, lean, delicate, weak, feeble; fine, attenuated, thin (cloth, paper, or liquid), slender; sharp, tapering; rarefied, subtle
more: n. m. The descendant of the founder of a village; the headman of a village.

v.t. To make thin, &c.; to sharpen (a pencil, &c.); to rarefy, to liquefy.

n. m. 1. A log or beam used as a harrow. 2. A large flat-bottomed boat. 3. A stone; a slab; a slate. 4. A long coarse grass of which mats are made, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poor; also, the usual fodder for elephants.
s. m. A pan, a pot.

more: v. a. 1. To exact, to subdue. 2. To beat.

more: n. m. A sinner. Tulsī, aise patit ko bār bār dhirkār! O Tulsī, out on such a sinner great a thousand
adj. Fallen, abject; guilty, abandoned. پتت پاون patit-pāwan, Purifying the guilty. (An epithet of the deity).
subs: adj. Purifying the guilty, an epithet of the Deity.

s.m. Belief, confidence, trust, reliance, dependence.

s. m. Trust, confidence, belief, dependence.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. pat, 'leaf.' s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.

n. f. Falling of leaves; autumn. Baiṭhe the parind ān-ke jab tak thā harā rūkh, Ab kyā hai jo pat-jhaṛ huā, aur jaṛ bhī gaī sūkh . On old age. Nazīr. Fairies would perch upon its branches when the tree was green, Naught's left but withered fallen leaves, the root all dried up e' en. pattā lag'nā , v. n. To be spoiled (a fruit). patiyā'ī , adj. ( Bot .) Leaf-like; fabaceous.
1. adj. Without leaves. پت جهڙهونا pāt jhaṛ honā, To lose itʼs leaves in autumn (a tree); ( met. ) To decay (a person from old age). 2. s. f. The fall of the leaf; autumn.
s. f. (dakh.) Fall of the leaf, autumn.
s.m. A cloth worn round the waist, girdle, sash, belt; a layer of mortar in a brick wall
more: s. m. A cloth worn round the waist, a girdle. زري پٿکا zarī paṭkā, The golden girdle (an ensign conferred by the Peshwā on generals who are invested with authority immediately by him). پٿکاباندهنا paṭkā bāndhnā, To determine on, or prepare for any act. پٿکاپکڙنا paṭkā pakaṛnā, To hinder, to obstruct.
subs: (-par), To gird up the loins (for), to prepare (for), or determine (on, an act, enterprise, &c.)
(-kā), 'To seize the belt (of); to hinder, hold back, prevent, obstruct.

def: v. n. To fall from oneʼs rank or estimation.
= paṭkā, q.v.
more: n. m. S. पट cloth. 1. A cloth worn round the waist; a girdle; sash; belt; zone. Deho gārī jitnā man āve jī; Ham leboṅ, Shām, paṭukā pasāri . E. Song. Heap on, Krishna, all wanton names upon my head, I'll take it all, all in my girdle here outspread. 2. A layer of mortar in a brick wall.

def: E. n. f. 1. Twitches; tweezes; aches; pains. Bukhār jātā rahā, paṭkan rah gaī . 2. A smart; a blow. 3. Disquiet; uneasiness.

more: (see پٿکنا) s. f. A knock, a fall. پٿکن کهانا paṭkan khānā, To fall down.

subs: (-ko), paṭkanā sunānā, To dash (one) down, to give a violent fall (in wrestling)
v.n. To have a severe fall, be thrown violently (in wrestling); to be knocked down.

more: v. a. See پٿکنا paṭaknā, sign. 1.

more: v. a. See پٿکنا paṭaknā, sign. 1.

adj. (f. -ī), Spare, emaciate, lean, delicate, weak, feeble; fine, attenuated, thin (cloth, paper, or liquid), slender; sharp, tapering; rarefied, subtle
more: adj. S. प्रतनु. 1. Fine; attenuated; wire-drawn; thin (cloth, paper, or liquid); fine-spun. Phr.—Thin as thread paper. 2. Purified; rare; subtle. 3. Lean; emaciated; meagre; delicate; weak. Phr.—Thin as a lath; lean as a rake; no more than a skeleton. 4. Narrow; slender; thread-like; contracted; spare. 5. Sharp; pointed; tapering. patlī chīz , n. ( Phys .) A fluid body. patlā hāl , n. m. Straitened circumstances; bad plight or condition; deplorable state. Aj-kal uskā baṛā patlā hāl hai. patlā karnā, patlānā , v. a. To rarefy; liquefy. 2. To thin; scrape; file; rasp. 3. To render narrow or contracted.
adj. m. 1. Fine, thin (cloth or liquids). 2. Lean, meagre, delicate, weak.
subs: s.f. A thin thing; a subtle substance; a fluid body
s.m. Straitened circumstances, bad plight or condition
v.t. To make thin, &c.; to sharpen (a pencil, &c.); to rarefy, to liquefy.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pattala, “thin”), from Sanskrit पत्त्रल (pattralá, “leafy”). Cognate with Gujarati પાતળું (pātaḷũ), Marathi पातळ (pātaḷ), Odia ପାତଳ (pātaḷa), Bengali পাতলা (patola), Assamese পাতল (patol).

def: s.f. = patlā-pan, q.v. s.m. Leanness, emaciation, thinness, fineness, attenuation, minuteness, meagreness, delicateness, weakness; wateriness; subtleness ( = patlāʼī).

I like black pants the most.
wety: Borrowed from English pantaloon, borrowed from French pantalon, borrowed from Italian Pantalone, from Ancient Greek Παντελεήμων (Panteleḗmōn), from πᾶς (pâs) + ἐλεήμων (eleḗmōn).

def: v.n. To be covered, be roofed, be thatched, be boarded; to fill, be filled up or closed, be made level; to be watered or irrigated (fields, &c.); to be full, be piled up, to abound, swarm, be thronged or crowded, be glutted (a market): cf. paṭānā, and pāṭnā
more: 1. v. n. To be paid, to be procured. 2. To be watered. 3. To fill. 4. To be covered, to be roofed, to be piled, &c. intrans. of پاٿنا q. v. 5. s. m. Name of a city. (Sans. पट्टन ).

ety: paṭ˚ = Prk. पट्टे(इ), or पट्ट(इ), fr. S. पट्ट
def: v.n. To be paid or discharged, be repaid or remitted; to be procured; to be accepted or honoured (a bill); to be admitted, passed, received as good; to do well, flourish, be carried on satisfactorily, be executed creditably or satisfactorily (a business); to agree, to be closed or struck (a bargain)
v. a. 1. To lay across; to make a boarded floor or roof; to roof; cover. 2. To pile; heap up; accumulate.
s.m. Ora vulvœ.
more: n. m. The margin of the pudendum muliebre .
v. n. S. पिठ् to hurt. To be beaten; to get a beating.

v. n. 1. To be paid ( paṭānā , 1.). 2. To be irrigated. Khet pānī se paṭ gayā . 3. To be boarded ( paṭānā , 3). Chhat paṭ gaī . 4. To agree together; to be struck or closed (a bargain). Saudā paṭ gayā . 5. To be glutted, crammed, choked. Āmoṅ se bāzār paṭā paṛā hai . 6. To be thronged, crowded; to swarm. 7. To be repaid, remitted. paṭtī hunḍī , n. f. A good bill.

def: v.t. To lay planks, &c. (across), to board (a floor); to roof, cover, shut or close in; to fill, fill up (a well, hole, ditch, &c.); to overstock, heap, pile, accumulate; — to water, irrigate; to pay.
more: v. a. 1. To roof, to cover, to shut. 2. To fill, fill up, overstock, heap, pile, accumulate. 3. To water, irrigate. 4. To pay.
